findings.title: Duplicate syncEngine.start() on auto-sync re-enable
findings.summary: handleToggle persists the pref (dispatch settingsUpdated) AND calls syncEngine.start(user.id); useSyncLifecycle's settingsUpdated listener then also calls start() + fullSync(). Start is idempotent (_started flag), but a duplicate fullSync is triggered only from the lifecycle path, so the direct start() call in handleToggle is redundant. Remove the explicit start/stop from handleToggle and rely on useSyncLifecycle as the single lifecycle owner.

findings.title: handleSyncNow sets status='syncing' but syncEngine.fullSync may also manage status
findings.summary: SyncSection.handleSyncNow manually writes setStatus('syncing'), then awaits syncEngine.fullSync(), then markSyncComplete(). If fullSync internally sets status (as SyncStatusIndicator.handleRetry also does), there is a duplicated lifecycle contract across three callers. Centralize status transitions inside syncEngine.fullSync() or provide a wrapper so UI callers do not have to remember to setStatus+markSyncComplete.

findings.title: handleSyncNow useCallback dependency list omits user
findings.summary: handleSyncNow is memoized on [busy] only. It does not reference user, which is correct today, but future edits that pass user.id into fullSync (multi-device token refresh) would silently capture a stale user ref. Non-blocking nit; consider adding a comment documenting why user is excluded.

findings.title: E2E test 'Sync Now shows loading state' relies on unexposed window shims
findings.summary: The test references window.__syncEngine__, window.__resetLocalData__, and window.__syncStatusStore__ shims that do not appear to be exposed anywhere in the codebase (only __authStore and __syncStatusStore are set up by existing stores). The test therefore skips its mock injection entirely and falls through to real engine behavior. Either expose the missing shims in dev/test builds, or rewrite the test to drive state through the public store API.

findings.title: Settings nav 'Sync' entry visible when signed out may confuse users
findings.summary: SyncSection returns null when signed out, but the nav pill/item for 'Sync' stays visible (per plan decision for discoverability). Clicking the nav yields an empty right pane with no explanatory state. Consider adding a minimal signed-out placeholder ('Sign in to manage cloud sync') inside SyncSection or gating the nav entry when useAuthStore.user is null.

findings.title: computeTotalSyncedItems re-runs on every lastSyncAt tick, even on no-op syncs
findings.summary: Effect is gated on lastSyncAt, so every markSyncComplete re-counts every non-skip table. For a user with ~20 tables and tens of thousands of rows, a Promise.all of count()s runs on every 30s nudge cycle. Consider memoizing by lastSyncAt reference equality plus debouncing, or computing counts only when the panel is visible.
